RLC 2026 Talk Schedule

No talks match your search.

Sun, August 16

11:00 AM

Track 1

Room B-2305

Theory of RL

  1. 1. Natural Policy Gradient for Bayesian Network Policies in Markov Potential Games
  2. 2. StaQ: a Finite Memory Approach to Discrete Action Policy Mirror Descent
  3. 3. Near-Optimal Reinforcement Learning for Linear Distributionally Robust Markov Decision Processes
  4. 4. Intrinsic Closed-Loop Practical Asymptotic Stability in Standard Reinforcement Learning
  5. 5. Provable Distributional Value Iteration under Partial Observability
  6. 6. Online KL-Regularized Reinforcement Learning with Function Approximation under Misspecification
  7. 7. Strategically Robust Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning with Linear Function Approximation
  8. 8. The ODE Method for Stochastic Approximation and Reinforcement Learning with Markovian Noise

Track 2

Room B-0325

Reward functions

  1. 1. Sign-SZPO: Provable Preference-based Reinforcement Learning with an Unknown Link Function
  2. 2. Reward-Conditioned Attention: How Reward Design Shapes What Autonomous Driving Agents See
  3. 3. Reward Design Agent for Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Multi-Modal, Multi-Environment Machine Teaching for Robust Reward Learning
  5. 5. Escaping Offline Pessimism: Vector-Field Reward Shaping for Safe Frontier Exploration
  6. 6. Leveraging Reward Machines for Efficient Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning
  7. 7. SR-Reward: Taking The Path More Traveled
  8. 8. From Novelty to Imitation: Self-Distilled Rewards for Offline Reinforcement Learning

Track 3

Room B-2325

Core RL algorithms

  1. 1. Direct Advantage Estimation for Scalable and Sample-efficient Deep Reinforcement Learning
  2. 2. Gradient Iterated Temporal-Difference Learning
  3. 3. Representation Regularization in Distributional Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. From Pixels to Factors: Learning Independently Controllable State Variables for Reinforcement Learning
  5. 5. Learning World Value Functions with Successor Representation and Vision Models
  6. 6. Gated Q-learning: Add Off-Policy Bias to Taste
  7. 7. Efficient Heteroscedastic Bayesian Optimization for Risk-Aware AutoRL
  8. 8. On-Policy Policy Gradient Reinforcement Learning Without On-Policy Sampling

Track 4

Room B-0305

Evaluation, benchmarks, and envs

  1. 1. The Yokai Learning Environment: Tracking Beliefs Over Space and Time
  2. 2. The Cell Must Go On: Agar.io for Continual Reinforcement Learning
  3. 3. SegDAC: Visual Generalization in Reinforcement Learning via Dynamic Object Tokens
  4. 4. Towards Affordable Energy: A Gymnasium Environment for Electric Utility Demand-Response Program
  5. 5. Assistax: A Multi-Agent Hardware-Accelerated Reinforcement Learning Benchmark for Assistive Robotics
  6. 6. The Open Ant: A Robot Platform for Reinforcement Learning Research
  7. 7. ARLBench: Flexible and Efficient Benchmarking for Hyperparameter Optimization in Reinforcement Learning

Mon, August 17

10:20 AM

Track 1

Room B-2305

Bandits

  1. 1. Randomized Exploration for Linear Bandits via Absolute Perturbations
  2. 2. ContrastSpanner: Learning Low-Rank Causal Contrasts to Alleviate Power-Set and Eluder Barriers
  3. 3. Learning with Coupled Uncertainty
  4. 4. Best-of-Both-Worlds Multi-Dueling Bandits: Unified Algorithms for Stochastic and Adversarial Preferences under Condorcet and Borda Objectives
  5. 5. Offline-to-Online Learning in Linear Bandits
  6. 6. Annealed Softmax Greedy in Many-Armed Bayesian Bandits
  7. 7. Collaborative Learning under Strategic Behavior: Mechanisms for Eliciting Feedback in Principal-Agent Bandit Games
  8. 8. Bandits for Efficient Experimentation: Adapting to Control Group, Preferences, and Context Drifts

Track 2

Room B-0325

Fairness, interpretability, and human-AI interaction + Hierarchical RL and skills

  1. 1. Limits of reinforcement learning for decision trees in Markov decision processes
  2. 2. Planning for Signaling in Low-Trust Environments
  3. 3. Inference-Time Policy Alignment for Fair Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Improving Human Performance with Value-Aware Interventions: A Case Study in Chess
  5. 5. Toward Agents That Reason About Their Computation
  6. 6. Let it Cook: Learning to Wait in Sequential Decision Making
  7. 7. Trajectory First: A Curriculum for Discovering Diverse Policies
  8. 8. Hierarchical Behaviour Spaces

Track 3

Room B-2325

Core RL algorithms

  1. 1. Overcoming Valid Action Suppression in Unmasked Policy Gradient Algorithms
  2. 2. PPO+: Enhancing proximal policy optimization
  3. 3. Repetition as Reinforcement: Enhancing Sample Efficiency via Instant Episode Repetition in Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Preventing Learning Stagnation in PPO by Scaling to 1 Million Parallel Environments
  5. 5. Delightful Policy Gradient
  6. 6. Counterfactual Shapley Credit Assignment
  7. 7. Reinforcement Learning for Stochastic Shortest Paths with Dead-Ends
  8. 8. Approximate Next Policy Sampling: Replacing Conservative Target Policy Updates in Deep RL

Track 4

Room B-0305

Applied RL

  1. 1. Deep Reinforcement Learning for Spacecraft Attitude Control During Atmospheric Re-Entry
  2. 2. Shielded Controller Units for RL with Operational Constraints Applied to Remote Microgrids
  3. 3. Maximum Entropy Behavior Exploration for Sim2Real Zero-Shot Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Building2Building: A Large Scale Benchmark for Generalizable Real-World Reinforcement Learning
  5. 5. Dynamic Object Masks as Goal Representations for Visual Goal-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning
  6. 6. Human-Like Goalkeeping in a Realistic Football Simulation: a Sample-Efficient Reinforcement Learning Approach
  7. 7. When Do We Need LLMs? A Diagnostic for Language-Driven Bandits
  8. 8. Discovering High Quality Chess Puzzles with Offline Reinforcement Learning

11:40 AM

Track 1

Room B-2305

Understanding deep RL

  1. 1. Learning When to Stop: Prefix-Optimal Dynamic Diffusion Policies for Continuous Control
  2. 2. Learning in Low-Dimensional Subspaces: Orthogonal Bottlenecks for Reinforcement Learning
  3. 3. Architecture over Algorithms: Network Modernization Improves Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Revisiting FTA: A Sparse One-to-Many Activation for Reinforcement Learning
  5. 5. Short-Term-to-Long-Term Memory Transfer for Knowledge Graphs under Partial Observability
  6. 6. Learning the Supports for Categorical Critic in Reinforcement Learning
  7. 7. Rethinking the Suitability of RL Algorithms Under Practical Transfer Constraints
  8. 8. V-Simba: Unleashing the Architectural Potential of RL in Visual Continuous Control

Track 2

Room B-0325

Task specification and reward functions

  1. 1. From Demonstrations to Rewards: Test-Time Prompt Optimization for VLM Reward Models
  2. 2. ICPL: Few-shot In-context Preference Learning via LLMs
  3. 3. Modification-Considering Value Learning for Reward Hacking Mitigation in RL
  4. 4. PB²: Preference Space Exploration via Population-Based Methods in Preference-Based Reinforcement Learning
  5. 5. Discovering Reinforcement Learning Interfaces with Large Language Models
  6. 6. Generalization in Monitored Markov Decision Processes (Mon-MDPs)
  7. 7. Design Principles for Tabular Multi-Policy MORL in Infinite Horizons
  8. 8. Grounding LTL Tasks in Sub-Symbolic RL Environments for Zero-Shot Generalization

Track 3

Room B-2325

Planning and model-based RL

  1. 1. Biased Dreams: Limitations to Epistemic Uncertainty Quantification in Latent Space Models
  2. 2. Risk-Aware General-Utility Markov Decision Processes
  3. 3. Stable Planning through Aligned Representations in Model-Based Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Bi-Level Reinforcement Learning Pathway for Sim-to-Real Optimality
  5. 5. Using Common Random Numbers for Simulation-based Planning with Rollouts
  6. 6. Gaussian Process Aggregation for Root-Parallel Monte Carlo Tree Search with Continuous Actions
  7. 7. Strategically-Linked Decisions in Long-Term Planning and Reinforcement Learning
  8. 8. When to Plan: Learning to Select Between Reactive Control and Deliberative Planning

Track 4

Room B-0305

Multi-agent RL

  1. 1. Centralized Adaptive Sampling for Reliable Co-training of Independent Multi-Agent Policies
  2. 2. Beyond Local Views: Global State Inference with Diffusion Models for Cooperative MARL
  3. 3. Credit Assignment and Focused Exploration for Sparse-reward Multi-agent Deep Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning with Reward Machines for Mixed Cooperative-Competitive Environments
  5. 5. Learning Multi-Agent Communication Protocol: Study on Information Entropy Efficiency in MARL
  6. 6. Coordination Graphs for Constrained Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
  7. 7. The challenge of hidden gifts in multi-agent reinforcement learning
  8. 8. Sociodynamics of Reinforcement Learning

Tue, August 18

10:20 AM

Track 1

Room B-2305

Theory of RL

  1. 1. On the Variance of Temporal Difference Learning and its Reduction Using Control Variates
  2. 2. Towards Formalizing Reinforcement Learning Theory: A Robbins-Siegmund Approach
  3. 3. Statistical Inference for Policy Evaluation with Temporal Difference Learning
  4. 4. Finite Time Analysis of the Natural Policy Gradient in Finite-Horizon Markov Decision Processes
  5. 5. Solvable models of learning to pursue a moving target
  6. 6. Boltzmann Rationality: An Axiomatic Characterization of Entropy-Regularized Policies
  7. 7. Optimal Regret for Policy Optimization in Average Reward MDPs Without Mixing
  8. 8. When Can Pure Exploitation Succeed in Linear RL? Decoys and Self-Identifiability for Greedy LSVI

Track 2

Room B-0325

Safe, robust, and risk-sensitive RL

  1. 1. On the Sample Complexity of Discounted Reinforcement Learning with Optimized Certainty Equivalents
  2. 2. Conformal Preemption of Failures in Sequential Decision-Making Agents
  3. 3. Distributionally Robust Self Paced Curriculum Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Adaptive Critic Shaping for Reinforcement Learning with Temporal Logic Constraint
  5. 5. Uncertainty-Aware Predictive Safety Filters for Probabilistic Neural Network Dynamics
  6. 6. An Unreasonably Simple Approach to Safe Reinforcement Learning
  7. 7. Skill-based Safe Reinforcement Learning with Risk Planning
  8. 8. V-OCBF: Learning Safety Filters from Offline Data via Value-Guided Offline Control Barrier Functions

Track 3

Room B-2325

Offline RL

  1. 1. Yes, Q-learning Helps Offline In-Context RL
  2. 2. CODA: Coordination via On-Policy Diffusion for Multi-Agent Offline Reinforcement Learning
  3. 3. Fully Offline Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. Dynamics Models for Offline Hyperparameter Selection in Real-World RL
  5. 5. Safe Flow Q-Learning: Offline Safe Reinforcement Learning with Reachability-Based Flow Policies
  6. 6. Conservative Value Priors: A Bayesian Path to Offline Reinforcement Learning
  7. 7. Offline RL with Hierarchical Action Chunking
  8. 8. {MOORL}: A Framework for Integrating Offline-Online Reinforcement Learning

Track 4

Room B-0305

Evaluation, benchmarks, and environments

  1. 1. Confidence Intervals for the Interquartile Mean
  2. 2. Synthetic Monitoring Environments for Reinforcement Learning
  3. 3. Memory Retention Is Not Enough to Master Memory Tasks in Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. PGTG: Procedurally Generated Grid-Based Traffic Gym
  5. 5. Ludax: A GPU-Accelerated Description Language for Board Games
  6. 6. Prediction-Based Markov Violation Scores for Detecting Non-Markovian Observations in Reinforcement Learning
  7. 7. NutriRL: A Benchmark for Nutritional Regulation under Delayed State Transitions
  8. 8. Physical Atari: A Robust and Accessible Platform for Real-time Reinforcement Learning on Robots

11:40 AM

Track 1

Room B-2305

Understanding deep RL

  1. 1. DART: Dual Adaptive Residual Tracking for Low-Bias Advantage Estimation and Credit Assignment in AI Agents
  2. 2. FlowRL: A Taxonomy and Modular Framework for Reinforcement Learning with Diffusion Policies
  3. 3. A Simple Baseline for Learning Approximate State Abstractions in Factored State Spaces
  4. 4. Endpoint Replay: Compressing the Recency Buffer in Deep Reinforcement Learning
  5. 5. Improving Reward-Based Hindsight Credit Assignment
  6. 6. Unsupervised Behavioral Compression: Learning Low-Dimensional Policy Manifolds through State-Occupancy Matching
  7. 7. Cohering Reinforcement Learning
  8. 8. A Survey of State Representation Learning for Deep Reinforcement Learning

Track 2

Room B-0325

RL fine-tuning of LLMs/VLMs/VLAs + Imitation learning

  1. 1. Temporally Extended Mixture-of-Experts Models
  2. 2. Simple Recipe Works: Vision-Language-Action Models are Natural Continual Learners with Reinforcement Learning
  3. 3. A-3PO: Accelerating Asynchronous LLM Training with Staleness-aware Proximal Policy Approximation
  4. 4. Scalable Causal Imitation Learning
  5. 5. Supervised Reward Inference
  6. 6. Minimal Ingredients for Reward Assignment from Expert Demonstrations
  7. 7. Generalize and Guide: Decomposing Rewards for Few-Shot Inverse Reinforcement Learning
  8. 8. Q-Based Variational Inverse Reinforcement Learning

Track 3

Room B-2325

Continual RL + Streaming RL + Exploration

  1. 1. Extending Differential Temporal Difference Methods for Episodic Problems
  2. 2. Revisiting Adam for Streaming Reinforcement Learning
  3. 3. Forager: a lightweight testbed for continual learning with partial observability in RL
  4. 4. Dense and Diverse Goal Coverage in Multi Goal Reinforcement Learning
  5. 5. Maximum-Entropy Exploration with Future State-Action Visitation Measures
  6. 6. Training on Irrelevant States Implies Data Augmentation: Generalization in Contextual MDPs
  7. 7. Weight a moment: risk-aware exploration with Bayes
  8. 8. Maximum Entropy Exploration Without Rollouts

Track 4

Room B-0305

Multi-agent RL

  1. 1. An Agent-Centric Dynamical Systems Perspective on Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
  2. 2. ASALT: Adaptive State Alignment for Lateral Transfer in Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning
  3. 3. A Causality-Inspired Spatial-Temporal Return Decomposition Approach for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
  4. 4. SCoUT: Scalable Communication via Utility-Guided Temporal Grouping in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
  5. 5. Decentralized Asymmetric DQN: Decentralization without Factorization in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
  6. 6. Fixing Incomplete Value Function Decomposition for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
  7. 7. Learning Communication Skills in Multi-task Multi-agent Deep Reinforcement Learning
  8. 8. ACPO: Agent-Chained Policy Optimization for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
